t1.answer:
## THE SITUATION
The Telangana government faces scrutiny and potential backlash from various factions due to the release of the caste survey findings, which have triggered criticism and highlighted disparities in land ownership among different castes. The survey's results, such as the Reddy community owning 13.5% of the land despite constituting only 4.8% of the population, may exacerbate existing social and political tensions. The fact that 12 lakh people opted for the 'No Caste' option in the survey also suggests a significant segment of the population may be distancing themselves from traditional caste affiliations.

## WHAT THE EVIDENCE SHOWS
① Telangana caste survey findings trigger criticism — Telangana Today — 16 Apr 2026: This article implies that the survey's findings have sparked controversy and criticism, potentially threatening the government's social and political stability. 
② 12 lakh people in Telangana opt for ‘No Caste’ in SEEEPC Survey-2024 — The Hindu — 17 Apr 2026: This indicates a notable trend of people rejecting traditional caste identities, which could have significant social and political implications.
③ Telangana caste survey shows Reddys at 4.8% population, own 13.5% land — Edex Live — 17 Apr 2026: This article highlights the disproportionate land ownership by the Reddy community, which may fuel resentment and demands for reform from other castes.

## THE PART NOT IN THE REPORTS
The coverage is thin on the potential responses or reactions from the Reddy community or other castes that may feel threatened or disadvantaged by the survey's findings. Typically, one would expect to see statements or actions from these groups, either accepting the findings and pledging to work towards more equitable land distribution or rejecting the survey's methodology and results. The absence of such reports suggests either a lack of immediate reaction or a gap in media coverage of these perspectives.

## TRAJECTORY
In the next 7-14 days, the situation is likely to escalate, with various caste groups and political parties reacting to the survey's findings. The government may face increasing pressure to address the disparities highlighted by the survey, potentially leading to announcements of policy reforms or initiatives aimed at reducing caste-based inequalities. The 'No Caste' trend may also gain more attention, with discussions on its implications for the state's social fabric and political landscape.

## CONFIDENCE: MEDIUM
The confidence in this assessment is medium because, while the survey's findings and the initial reactions provide a clear indication of the underlying tensions and potential for conflict, the lack of comprehensive coverage of all relevant stakeholders' responses introduces some uncertainty about how the situation will unfold.

articles.title: 12 lakh people in Telangana opt for ‘No Caste’ in SEEEPC Survey-2024
articles.text_snippet:
Nearly 12 lakh people in Telangana opted for the ‘No Caste’ option in the Telangana Socio Economic, Educational, Employment, Political and Caste Survey (SEEEPC) 2024.
Of the 3,54,77,554 people who responded in the survey, this was 3.4% of the population in the State. The survey is colloquially referred to as Caste Census.
Published - April 17, 2026 07:19 am IST

Phases

articles.text_snippet:

articles.text_snippet:
97.38 pc Delhi students clear CBSE Class 10 board exams
97.38 pc Delhi students clear CBSE Class 10 board exams
New Delhi, The capital recorded an overall pass percentage of 97.38 per cent in CBSE's Class 10 board exams, marking an improvement from 95.14 per cent logged last year.
The Central Board of Secondary Education on Wednesday announced the results for its Class 10 board examinations. Delhi government schools registered a pass percentage of 93.68 per cent and 91.3 per cent in the east and west zones, respectively.

followups: What are the warning signs to watch in the next 14 days?
How is the opposition responding to this situation?
What should the administration prepare for?

t2.answer:
CURRENT POSITIONING
The opposition in Telangana is currently using the caste survey findings to criticize the government, with some politicians condemning statements made by BJP MP Tejasvi Surya regarding the division of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. The stated position of the opposition is to highlight the disparities in land ownership and population among different castes, as revealed by the survey. However, their revealed position suggests that they are trying to gain political mileage by criticizing the government and the BJP.

PATTERN ANALYSIS
The consistent pattern in the opposition's strategy is to use the caste survey findings to create a narrative of social inequality and to blame the government for it. This pattern suggests that the opposition is trying to appeal to the marginalized sections of society and to create a sense of discontent among the people. The pattern can be explicitly named as "Social Inequality Narrative".

THE AUDIENCE THEY ARE PLAYING TO
The opposition is playing to the marginalized sections of society, particularly the backward castes and the landless laborers. They are trying to create a sense of discontent among these groups and to mobilize them against the government.

PRESSURE POINTS
The opposition is conspicuously not talking about the fact that the caste survey was conducted by the Congress government in Telangana, which suggests that they are trying to avoid blame for the disparities revealed by the survey. They are also not talking about the fact that the survey shows that the Reddy community, which constitutes 4.8% of the population, owns 13.5% of the land in the state, which suggests that they are trying to avoid antagonizing this powerful community.

WHAT TO WATCH
1. Whether the opposition will continue to use the caste survey findings to criticize the government, or if they will shift their focus to other issues.
2. How the government will respond to the opposition's criticism, and whether they will try to counter the narrative of social inequality.
3. Whether the marginalized sections of society will be mobilized by the opposition's narrative, and whether they will become a significant political force in the state.

EVIDENCE
[1] Telangana caste survey findings trigger criticism — Telangana Today — 16 Apr 2026
[2] ಕಾಂಗ್ರೆಸ್ ಅವಧಿಯಲ್ಲಿ ಆಂಧ್ರ ವಿಭಜನೆ ಭಾರತ-ಪಾಕ್ ವಿಭಜನೆಗಿಂತ ಕೆಟ್ಟದಾಗಿತ್ತು- ತೇಜಸ್ವಿ ಸೂರ್ಯ ವಾಗ್ದಾಳಿ; ತೆಲಂಗಾಣ ರಾಜಕಾರಣಿಗಳಿಂದ ಟೀಕೆ — Kannada Prabha — 16 Apr 2026
[4] Telangana caste survey shows Reddys at 4.8% population, own 13.5% land — Edex Live — 17 Apr 2026

New Delhi: BJP MP Tejasvi Surya today took a swipe at the Congress in his Lok Sabha speech, criticising the Congress party for dividing a state in the same way as the British divided India and Pakistan. 

In 2014, the Congress party divided the state of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana in a way that was worse than how the British divided India and Pakistan, Tejasvi Surya claimed.

followups: What is their likely next move?
What are their key vulnerabilities right now?
How has their strategy shifted over the last month?

t3.answer:
THE SIGNAL CLUSTER
The signals that suggest a developing situation in Telangana include: 
1. The Telangana government's ban on plastic flowers (Signal 1: MEDIUM) - This move, while environmentally friendly, may have unforeseen economic and social implications that could lead to unrest.
2. The Telangana caste survey findings triggering criticism (Signal 2: HIGH) - The controversy surrounding the survey's findings could exacerbate existing social tensions and create a volatile political environment.
3. The growth of aerospace and defense exports in Telangana (Signal 3: LOW) - While this is a positive economic development, it may also create new security concerns or dependencies that could impact the government's risk profile.

The combination of these signals changes the risk calculation because it suggests that the government is facing both social and economic challenges that could impact its stability and decision-making.

PRECEDENT CHECK
There is no clear precedent in the provided corpus for this specific combination of events. However, the controversy surrounding the caste survey findings is reminiscent of other social and political tensions that have arisen in India in the past.

THE THRESHOLD QUESTION
If the criticism of the caste survey findings escalates into widespread protests or violence within the next two weeks, that would confirm a high-risk trajectory for the government. Specifically, if opposition parties or social groups organize large-scale demonstrations in Hyderabad, the government would need to respond quickly to prevent the situation from spiraling out of control.

THE SILENCE SIGNALS
The lack of coverage on the potential economic impact of the plastic flower ban is notable. This suggests that the government may not be fully prepared to address the potential consequences of this decision, which could lead to unforeseen problems.

RISK RATING: MEDIUM
The risk rating is medium because while there are potential flashpoints, such as the caste survey controversy, the government has also taken proactive steps, like the plastic flower ban, that demonstrate its ability to address social and environmental issues. However, the combination of these factors creates uncertainty and the potential for unforeseen consequences.

MONITORING BRIEF
In the next 72 hours, monitor the following:
1. Any statements or actions from opposition parties in response to the caste survey findings.
2. Reports of protests or demonstrations related to the plastic flower ban or the caste survey.
3. Announcements from the government regarding measures to address the economic or social impacts of the plastic flower ban.

EVIDENCE
[1] Article 4: Telangana government bans plastic flowers.
[2] Article 9: Telangana caste survey findings trigger criticism.
[3] Article 6: Aerospace and defense exports overtake pharma in Telangana.

articles.title: Advanced labs soon in Delhi ITIs for future-ready skills
articles.text_snippet:
New Delhi: The Delhi government will equip its four industrial training institutes (ITIs) with high-tech labs to train youngsters in power electronics, electric vehicles and the automotive sector.
Chief minister Rekha Gupta said the initiative marks a significant step towards building a skilled, industry-ready workforce. "These modern labs will not only enhance technical capabilities but also give trainees exposure to real-world industrial practices, opening up opportunities for both employment and self-employment," she said.

articles.title: Aerospace, defence overtake pharma to form chunk of Telangana’s exports. Next stop: aero-engine capital
articles.text_snippet:
New Delhi: India’s youngest state Telangana is on course to becoming one of the country’s most vital anchors for aviation and defence with exports corresponding to these sectors accounting for a third of the state’s exports in the financial year 2025-2026.
For the first time since 2014 when Telangana was carved out of Andhra Pradesh, aerospace and defence exports have surged past the state’s pharma exports which held firm for more than five decades, the state’s socio-economic outlook released in March has revealed.
